name: Bhava-Ganesha
dates_notes: Direct disciple of Vijñānabhikṣu; dated by manuscript and by the colophons of the Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana and the Pradīpa on the Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya, both of which describe him as Bhikṣu's pupil.
core_thesis:
Bhāva-Gaṇeśa is the principal disciple of Vijñānabhikṣu and the figure through whom *avibhāga-advaita* receives its first scholastic transmission. The doctrinal architecture is taken over from Bhikṣu without revision: Brahman, *cit*, *acit*, and the plural *jīvas* are really differentiated during *sṛṣṭi* and really resolved into non-separation (*avibhāga*) at *pralaya* and *mokṣa* (*Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana*, opening *prakaraṇa*; *Pradīpa* on *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* 1.61–67); *avibhāga* is distinguished from Śaṅkara's *abheda*-from-eternity (*Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana*, *bheda-vicāra-prakaraṇa*); Sāṃkhya's *tattva*-analysis is true within the manifest order and crowned by Vedānta (*Pradīpa* on *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* upodghāta); *Īśvara* is admissible within Sāṃkhya (*Pradīpa* on *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* 1.92–94, following Bhikṣu's reading of *na-īśvara-siddhiḥ* constructively).

The contribution is exegetical-consolidating, not innovative. Two works carry it. The *Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana* is an independent prose *prakaraṇa* that re-states Bhikṣu's *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* positions in compact pedagogic form, restructured by topic (*pramāṇa*, Brahman, *jīva*, *jagat*, *bandha*, *mokṣa*) so that the school's settled positions can be taught in their own right rather than only through the Bhāṣya's *adhikaraṇa*-by-*adhikaraṇa* sequence (*Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana*, opening *upodghāta* announcing the work's pedagogic purpose). The *Pradīpa* is a sub-commentary on Vijñānabhikṣu's *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* that opens Bhikṣu's compressed glosses on the *Sāṃkhya-Sūtras*, fixing the school's reading of the contested *sūtras* (*Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* 1.92, on *na-īśvara-siddhiḥ*; 6.59 on the resolution at *pralaya*) at scholastic pitch.

The *Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana* in particular is the principal early-modern testimony that *avibhāga-advaita* survived Bhikṣu as a teachable position — that *avibhāga*, not *abheda* and not *bhedābheda*, names a distinct Vedāntic option which can be transmitted by direct discipleship. Without Bhāva-Gaṇeśa's two works the Bhikṣu-tradition would be attested only in Bhikṣu's own corpus and in Dasgupta's later reconstruction.

engaged_works.title: Tattva-Yatharthya-Dipana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored by Bhāva-Gaṇeśa per colophonic ascription in the surviving manuscripts; quoted as his work in later doxographic literature.
engaged_works.summary: An independent prose *prakaraṇa* setting out the *avibhāga-advaita* position topic by topic. The work opens with a programmatic *upodghāta* announcing its pedagogic purpose: to make Vijñānabhikṣu's *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* positions accessible without requiring the reader to follow the Bhāṣya through the entire Brahma-Sūtra. The structure is six-fold: *pramāṇa-prakaraṇa* (the *pramāṇa*-economy of the school, including the status of *śabda* against the Buddhist pramāṇa-vāda); *brahma-svarūpa-prakaraṇa* (Brahman as *sat-cit-ānanda* and as the *māyin* who possesses *māyā* identified with *prakṛti*, following Bhikṣu's *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 1.1.5); *jīva-prakaraṇa* (the *jīva* as real *aṃśa* of Brahman, neither identical from eternity nor permanently separate); *jagat-prakaraṇa* (the world as the genuine differentiated state of *prakṛti* during *sṛṣṭi*); *bandha-mokṣa-prakaraṇa* (bondage as continued *vibhāga* and liberation as achieved *avibhāga*); and *bheda-vicāra-prakaraṇa* (the technical distinction of *avibhāga* from Śaṅkara's *abheda*-from-eternity and from Bhāskara's *aupādhika-bheda*). The polemical engagement is Bhikṣu's: *māyā-vāda* is rejected as a deviation that secures non-dualism by reducing the world's reality, where the *avibhāga* construal secures non-dualism without that reduction (*Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana*, *brahma-svarūpa-prakaraṇa*, on *māyā* as *prakṛti* not *avidyā*). The *prakaraṇa* is the principal early-modern document by which *avibhāga-advaita* circulates as a transmissible position rather than as Bhikṣu's individual teaching.

engaged_works.title: Pradipa on Samkhya-Pravacana-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Vijñānabhikṣu's Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: The *Pradīpa* ("lamp") is Bhāva-Gaṇeśa's sub-commentary on Vijñānabhikṣu's *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya*. The method is *sūtra*-by-*sūtra* exposition of Bhikṣu's compressed glosses, with the school's settled position fixed at each contested point. Three loci do the load-bearing work. The *Pradīpa* on *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* 1.92–94 (the *na-īśvara-siddhiḥ* sūtra) follows Bhikṣu in reading the standard arguments for Sāṃkhya's atheism as turning on textual omission rather than on positive incompatibility; the conclusion is that classical Sāṃkhya can admit *Īśvara* without compromising the *prakṛti–puruṣa* analysis (*Pradīpa* ad loc.). The *Pradīpa* on *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* 1.61–67 fixes the *avibhāga*-reading of *pralaya*: the resolution of *prakṛti* and the *puruṣas* into Brahman is not their having-been-identical-all-along but their genuine non-separation as a returned state. The *Pradīpa* on *Sāṃkhya-Pravacana-Bhāṣya* 6.59 reads the final liberation-state of the *puruṣa* as *avibhāga* in the strong sense (non-separation from Brahman) rather than mere *kaivalya* (isolation from *prakṛti*) — the same move Bhikṣu makes in the *Yoga-Vārttika* on *Yoga-Sūtra* 4.34 and in the *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* on *Brahma-Sūtra* 4.4.1–4. The work is the school's fixing of how the *Sāṃkhya-Sūtras* are to be read once they have been integrated into *avibhāga-advaita*; with the *Tattva-Yāthārthya-Dīpana* it constitutes the first generation of post-Bhikṣu transmission.
